response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and systems developer, but he misses nearly every core requirement of this role: no Japanese proficiency (native-level required), no embedded networking or LAN-switch experience, no security-protocol background (MACsec, 802.1X, authentication systems), and no demonstrated Linux kernel analysis or modification work. His C/C++ experience is limited to a brief seven-month stint in 2014-2015, with his career since centered on web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang). The gap between his profile and the role's embedded security focus is too wide to justify an interview.
strengths: Broad, long-running engineering career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some hardware/robotics exposure
Low-latency systems experience (DLang, asynchronous I/O, high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby)
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship) and Linux familiarity
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed; the role requires native-level Japanese
No embedded networking, switch, or security-technology experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, tamper detection)
C for UNIX experience is minimal and dated, with no evidence of Linux kernel investigation or source modification
